channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Nvidia’s new AI model Fugatto can generate music and audio
item.description: Nvidia on Monday showed a new artificial intelligence model for generating music and audio that can modify voices and generate novel sounds — technology aimed at the producers of music, films and video games.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Free travel planner? Use AI for your next trip
item.description: I’m going to Europe soon, and planning my trip was a breeze. Yes, really! AI did a big chunk of the work for me — and it can help you plan your next getaway, too.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Khanna says Democrats will work with Musk's DOGE on cutting defense budget
item.description: Rep. Ro Khanna (D-Calif.) on Monday suggested that Democrats could work with tech billionaire Elon Musk’s “Department of Government Efficiency” (DOGE) on cutting the defense budget. The issue came up during an interview Khanna gave to CNN anchor Jim Acosta, who noted the new efficiency panel.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Most young knowledge workers using AI: Poll
item.description: The vast majority of Generation Z knowledge workers said they are using artificial intelligence (AI) tools in their work, according to a new study, underscoring the increased embrace of the advancing technology.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: The Evolution of Technology: Insights from Novanta's CIO and CISO, Sarah Betadam
item.description: A conversation with Sarah Betadam, CIO and CISO of Novanta, covering women in tech, career development, IT transformation, security, AI, and relationship building.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Intel’s CHIPS Act grant reduced as production delays and losses mount
item.description: The US government has scaled back Intel’s preliminary CHIPS Act grant from $8.5 billion to under $8 billion, reflecting concerns over the company’s delayed investments and financial woes, The New York Times reported.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: How to use iPhone's mic mode for crystal-clear, noise-free calls
item.description: Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson reveals how iPhone’s new Automatic Mic Mode adjusts settings for optimal call quality automatically.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 3 reasons why the US government should lean on data and AI efficiency
item.description: Elon Musk revolutionized products in the auto industry. No one brand since the Model T and Henry Ford has had such a profound impact on design thinking, efficiency, and innovation all running in parallel. Tesla cars can be updated 40 times more frequently than the giants in the industry.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Laws to Ban Children Under 16 from Social Media Platforms in Australia Delayed
item.description: Australian government-commissioned evaluation of age assurance technologies is completed, delaying laws to ban children under 16 from social media platforms.
item.dc:creator: Rod Mcguirk, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: SpaceX will deliver its first lunar terrain vehicle to the Moon by 2029
item.description: Hot on the heels of another productive test flight, two new lunar customers have been added to the flight manifest for SpaceX’s Starship. Eagle landing Lunar Outpost said on Thursday that SpaceX will deliver its first Lunar Terrain Vehicle, Eagle, to the Moon by 2029.
item.dc:creator: Tim Fernholz, Fast Company

item.title: Earth bids farewell to its temporary 'mini moon' that is possibly a chunk of our actual moon
item.description: Earth is parting company with an asteroid that's been tagging along as a “mini moon” for the past two months.
item.dc:creator: Marcia Dunn, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Gallagher not interested in serving as SEC chair in second Trump administration
item.description: Dan Gallagher, chief legal officer at the financial services firm Robinhood, said Friday that he is not interested in chairing the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) in President-elect Trump’s second administration.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Risk and Compliance Exchange 2024: DoD’ Stacy Bostjanick, DCMA’s Jennifer Henderson on finding ‘any means possible’ to help small biz with CMMC
item.description: As the Cybersecurity Maturity Model Certification program inches closer to reality, DoD prepares defense industrial base for compliance requirements.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Supreme Court drops Facebook’s appeal of investor suit in Cambridge Analytica scandal
item.description: The Supreme Court on Friday tossed Facebook’s appeal in a securities lawsuit against the social media giant, indicating the court shouldn’t have taken up the case. The unsigned, one-sentence opinion contained no explanation and said the case was “dismissed as improvidently granted.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, The Hill

item.title: Pentagon rolls out new strategy for private 5G
item.description: DoD will rely on existing commercial networks "to the maximum extent possible," but in some cases, will need to run its own 5G infrastructure. The post Pentagon rolls out new strategy for private 5G first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jared Serbu, Federal News Network

item.title: Thai court dismisses activist's suit against Israeli spyware producer over lack of evidence
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — A Thai court on Thursday threw out a lawsuit brought by a pro-democracy activist which alleged spyware produced by an Israeli tech firm had been used to hack his phone.
item.dc:creator: JERRY HARMER and DAVID RISING, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Red Hat Linux to be official WSL distro
item.description: Red Hat Enterprise Linux (RHEL) is set to become an official Linux distribution for Microsoft’s Windows Subsystem for Linux (WSL), a Windows feature that allows users to run Linux environments on Windows without a separate virtual machine.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: AI-powered dog robot sniffs out invasive fire ants
item.description: Fueled by artificial intelligence, CyberDog the robotic dog is designed specifically locate fire ant nests, which are endangering ecosystems.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Nvidia is Wall Street's most valuable company. How it got there, by the numbers
item.description: Nvidia has once again turned out quarterly results that exceeded Wall Street's forecasts. The company has seen soaring demand for its semiconductors, which are used to power artificial intelligence applications.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Comcast to spin off cable networks that were once the entertainment giant's star performers
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Comcast is spinning off into a new company many of the cable television networks that were once at the entertainment giant's heart as consumers increasingly swap out their cable TV subscriptions for streaming platforms.
item.dc:creator: MATT OTT and WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: US gathers allies to talk AI safety as Trump's vow to undo Biden's AI policy overshadows their work
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump has vowed to repeal President Joe Biden's signature artificial intelligence policy when he returns to the White House for a second term. What that actually means for the future of AI technology remains to be seen.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: You’ll soon be able to clone your voice to speak other languages ​​in Teams
item.description: In connection with this year’s Ignite conference, Microsoft has unveiled a new interpretation tool that will be added to Teams in the spring.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: IT Job Market Trends: Cloud Expertise and Security Know-How Remain Critical
item.description: Discover key trends and statistics related to the IT job market, including IT job growth, top-paying certifications, hiring plans, and challenges in acquiring new skills.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Must-do privacy settings on your iPhone in iOS 18.1
item.description: Apple's latest iOS update introduces advanced artificial intelligence capabilities, which may be capturing and analyzing sensitive information.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Microsoft pitches AI 'agents' that can perform tasks on their own at Ignite 2024
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella told customers at a conference in Chicago on Tuesday that the company is teaching a new set of artificial intelligence tools how to "act on our behalf across our work and life.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Forget driverless cars. One company wants autonomous helicopters to spray crops and fight fires
item.description: HENNIKER, N.H. (AP) — When Hector Xu was learning to fly a helicopter in college, he recalled having a few “nasty experiences” while trying to navigate at night.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: German police break up an environmental activists' camp near Tesla plant outside Berlin
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — German police broke up a protest camp housing environmental activists in a forest near the Tesla electric car factory in Grünheide outside Berlin on Tuesday, citing violations of public safety and order.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News
